title: koordersiodendron pinnatum
intro: Koordersiodendron pinnatum, commonly known as the "Malaysian Oak", is a species of plant in the family Celastraceae. This plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as therapeutic actions like wound healing and fever reduction. The bioactive constituents of Koordersiodendron pinnatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, wounds, and skin infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.

intro_study: According to "Indian journal of experimental biology", koordersiodendron pinnatum seeds have shown anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and anti-ulcerogenic activities in rats.

parts: Koordersiodendron pinnatum has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and bark, which are traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine. The roots of the Koordersiodendron pinnatum plant contain medicinal constituents like koordersione and koordersioside, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bark of the Koordersiodendron pinnatum plant is also used medicinally and contains terpenoids and phenolic compounds, including koordersin and koordersioside.
preparations: Koordersiodendron pinnatum has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used for medicinal purposes. A tea made from the plant is used to treat fever and skin conditions, while a tincture can be applied topically to alleviate pain and inflammation. An infusion of the leaves is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. side_effects: Koordersiodendron pinnatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, nausea, and dizziness due to its high toxicity levels. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also cause stomach problems, including diarrhea and vomiting. Moreover, improper use of Koordersiodendron pinnatum may lead to respiratory issues, such as shortness of breath and difficulty breathing.
